scenario_id.description: Unique, stable identifier within the run set. Also used as Control Center ScenarioName unless overridden. Never reuse after retiring a scenario.

tdm_ref.description: Overrides the run set's tdm_ref for this scenario only.

baseline_control_center.description: Overrides the run set's baseline_control_center for this scenario only.

input_files.description: Input file paths for this scenario, relative to the run_set directory. Resolved to absolute paths by the framework before writing the Control Center — keeps scenario YAMLs machine-independent.

overrides.description: Control Center key/value overrides specific to this scenario, applied on top of the run set's shared overrides.

variables.description: Documentation-only scenario parameters (e.g. a custom driver/step script's inputs) that are not Control Center keys and are not applied by the framework -- unlike overrides, which are validated against the baseline and rendered into _ControlCenter.yaml, these are not read by anything yet. Record here what a custom driver_script's companion step script is meant to consume once it reads them itself.

driver_script.description: Overrides the run set's driver_script for this scenario only (or the framework default, if the run set doesn't declare one). Path to a custom driver script, relative to the run_set directory. Staged keeping its own filename; companion scripts stay in place and must be referenced by a relative path back to their location.

start_from_copy.description: scenario_id of another scenario in this same run set whose most recent successful run's raw scenario folder should be copied (in full) into this scenario's folder before execution -- lets a scenario whose modification only affects a later model step skip recomputing everything upstream. Resolved via that scenario's run_metadata.json (works whether it was run via the CLI or imported from a manual run), skipping past any newer failed attempts to find the most recent success. Only used by run-scenario/run-set -- has no effect on manual execution.

lock_down_copy.description: When true, suppresses the start_from_copy copy for this scenario's runs, leaving its raw scenario folder untouched by seeding -- use once the folder already holds the seeded state it needs, since the raw folder is reused across every run attempt and would otherwise be re-copied (potentially tens of GB) on each retry. Has no effect unless start_from_copy is also declared; keep start_from_copy set for the record of where it was seeded from.

prep_script.description: Path to a Python prep script, relative to the run_set directory. Run before this scenario's model execution. Receives --run-set-dir and --scenario-id as arguments. Runs after any run_set-level prep_script.

datafile.description: Glob pattern, evaluated against each output file's path relative to the scenario folder.

columns.description: Column names (matched against the CSV's header row) to keep. The matched file is written as a column-filtered copy named '<stem>_filtered.csv' instead of a byte-for-byte copy -- for a large summary CSV where only a handful of columns are actually read downstream (e.g. Cube Voyager's 'TAZ-Based Metrics.csv', ~18 columns and ~200 MB, comfortably over any reasonable size ceiling). Omit to copy the matched file(s) byte-for-byte.

matrix.description: Glob pattern, evaluated against each output file's path relative to the scenario folder, matching a Cube Voyager .mtx matrix file.

tabs.description: Table/core names to extract from the matched .mtx file. Only these named tables are kept -- for large multi-table Cube matrices (e.g. skims, several hundred MB) where only one or two tables are actually needed downstream. Requires Voyager to be available at curation time (config/local.yaml's Voyager_EXE).

format.description: Output format for the extracted tables, written as '<stem>.<format>'. 'omx' (default): an OMX file, readable directly in Python via the openmatrix package. 'mtx': converted back to Cube's own native TPP matrix format via CONVERTMAT -- for when the curated output is meant to be read by Cube Voyager itself, not Python.

network.description: Glob pattern, evaluated against each output file's path relative to the scenario folder, matching a Cube Voyager .net network file.

fields.description: Link attribute field names to keep from the matched .net file (plus geometry, for shp/geojson formats) -- a .net file is Voyager's proprietary format and typically carries far more link attributes than a report needs. Requires Voyager to be available at curation time (config/local.yaml's Voyager_EXE).

format.description: Output format for the field-filtered network, written as '<stem>.<format>' ('shp' is written as a single '<stem>.shp.zip' bundling the shapefile's sidecar files). 'geojson' (default): readable directly in Python via geopandas, reprojected to EPSG:4326 (WGS84) to match GeoJSON convention and this codebase's own vt_CreateGeoJsons.py precedent. 'shp': a field-filtered Esri shapefile, zipped, kept in the source geometry's native CRS (no reprojection). 'net': Cube's own native .net format, field-filtered via NETWORK's EXCLUDE= -- for when the curated output is meant to be read by Cube Voyager itself, not Python/GIS tools.

manual_scenario_folder.description: Path to this scenario's raw output folder, relative to the TDM submodule root, when it was run manually outside run-scenario/run-set. Read by import-manual-run(-set) to locate outputs to curate; unused by a CLI-driven run.
